reviews.review_description: Such a beautiful vr media app.  The experience was amazing!!!. The graphics were top notch !! My only complaints are that it was entirely too short and I don't know when another media app like this will be released in the future.

reviews.review_title: Technical issues ruined it for me.
reviews.review_description:
Where is the option for a pure VR experience instead of a mixed reality mode that falls flat in trying to incorporate the 3d objects into your play space?
In 2024, why do we get 24p-ish framerate on the main character when she floats around?! Looks absolutely horrible.

This is the type of content I was expecting to come from Disney. A short, cute experience that my 5 year old grand-daughter was enthralled by. She played it 3 times in a row and each time the items were in slightly different places. But, the whole time, she is running around with the girl singing. I'm sure if she played it enough she would have memorized the lyrics. Could you imagine the thrill children would get with their favorite movie characters. I know Disney had some VR content back in the day, but they gave up. Please do more like this.
It's a very attractive app, not much to it, not something you'd keep on your headset unless you have children in the house and I think they'll play it a couple of times only before moving on.
It’s so beautiful and has a wonderful message. Disney should do this but then they’d charge a lot money. I’d like to see more of these VR/MR musicals.
I'd like to see more Musicals in VR. I ran this in a space that might have been smaller than the recommended area and that didn't do too well. But I got the idea for what they were trying to achieve.

Wonderful Spatial Audio, art style and music.
Great sharp graphics.

Doesn’t really utilize the spatial room data.
And is only 5minutes long!!!

But free. So get it!

This app ignores the Quest 3 feature that can make MR so immersive.

It asks you for permission to use your spatial data, yet ignores your spatial data. The app should know where my real windows are yet it ignored those and placed a window on a wall. The app should know where my bed is, but it placed a character half embedded in it. That’s when I quit.

The app also invites you to get up close to objects then leaves the guardian on so you can’t.

If you’re in a completely empty room with no windows and nothing hanging on the walls, you should give this a try since the one thing this app seems aware of are wall boundaries. The graphics are very nice.

If you want to see what an app can do that actually utilizes the Quest 3’s MR abilities download First Encounters.

This is a really nice and immersive mixed reality experience. It would be perfect to show someone who's new to VR, as it's very immersive and fun to walk and look around, and there's no controls to worry about. It's free, only lasts a few minutes, and I definitely recommend you try this!

There's one thing that I think would help with the immersion - if it could incorporate dynamic occlusion using the spatial data, and live dynamic occlusion. I'm no developer so I don't know how easy this would be.

VR/AR is absolutely the future of storytelling, in this experience shows exactly why. It makes you feel the magic of the epic Disney animated films times 100 since you are able to be inside of it and be a part of it. 

The music was beautiful, the animation was lovely and it was just a really cool experience. Please make more like this. I will gladly pay for a longer experience!!!

I stumbled on this app by accident and I’m sure glad I did! A short, but very sweet family friendly MR experience with easily some of the best visuals I’ve seen on Quest 3. Terrific use of spatial audio as well. A true testament to the magic of MR as an immersive story telling medium. Disney should take note!
